audit.aligned:
- Four stdio servers configured correctly: filesystem, github, memory, sqlite — all use `npx -y` pattern, correct `command`/`args` shape, env vars referenced via shell-style `${GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN}` substitution. - github server passes GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN via `env` block (correct pattern). - sqlite server uses explicit `--db-path` arg pointing to a persistent file.
- ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:5 is set — matches the `auto:<N>` threshold pattern documented under "Configure tool search" (5% context threshold, correctly tuned below the default 10%). - MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=600000 is set — controls per-tool timeout, consistent with docs recommendation to configure MCP_TIMEOUT for slow servers. - CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC=1 — reduces background calls, appropriate for high-volume MCP usage.
audit.gaps:
- All four MCP servers in claude_desktop_config.json use stdio (npx). No HTTP or SSE remote servers are configured in either config file. Docs recommend HTTP as the preferred transport for remote/cloud services; stdio is only appropriate for local processes. - SSE transport is now deprecated per docs ("Use HTTP servers instead, where available"). No SSE servers exist today, but any future remote additions should use `--transport http`.
- No project-scoped `.mcp.json` at /Users/alexzh/platform-manager root. Project-scoped servers enable team sharing via version control. The platform-manager plugin is team-distributed, so shared servers belong in `.mcp.json` rather than in the claude_desktop_config.json personal file. - All servers currently sit in claude_desktop_config.json (Desktop global scope), not in Claude Code scopes (local/project/user via `~/.claude.json`). Docs note `claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop` can migrate these, and scoping at `user` or `project` level is preferred for Claude Code workflows.
- No OAuth 2.0 configuration present for any server. Docs show `/mcp` command triggers OAuth flow for remote servers (Sentry, GitHub HTTP endpoint, Notion, etc.). The github server uses a PAT via env var, which is valid but less secure than OAuth for remote HTTP servers. - No `headersHelper` or static `headers` configured for any server — relevant if any future HTTP servers need non-OAuth auth (Kerberos, SSO tokens, short-lived creds).
- `MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS` not set in settings.json env block. Default cap is 25,000 tokens; the crawler/database workflows in this repo could easily exceed that. - MCP `list_changed` dynamic tool updates: no configuration or documentation of which servers support this; sqlite and filesystem servers likely do not, but worth verifying. - MCP resources (`@server:protocol://resource/path` @ mentions) not documented or demonstrated anywhere in the config/skills. - MCP prompts as commands (`/mcp__servername__promptname`) not leveraged in any skill. - `ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS` not set — defaults to enabled; if Claude.ai connectors are not wanted in Claude Code sessions, this should be explicitly set to `false`.
- No `managed-mcp.json` at macOS system path `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-mcp.json`. For a 5-surface ecosystem treated as enterprise, a managed config ensures all surfaces use the same approved server set. - No `allowedMcpServers` / `deniedMcpServers` policy in any settings file. Docs describe these for org-level governance; relevant given the multi-identity setup.
- The platform-manager plugin has no `.mcp.json` at plugin root. The plugin.json description promises "ecosystem hooks" but bundles no MCP servers. If neon-postgres or other data services should travel with the plugin, they need `.mcp.json` in the plugin root (not in claude_desktop_config.json).
audit.actions:
1. CREATE /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/.mcp.json Add project-scoped MCP servers that all surfaces should share (e.g. neon-postgres HTTP endpoint, any internal tooling). Use `${VAR:-default}` expansion for env-specific values. Commit to version control so all surfaces pick it up.
2. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/claude-desktop/claude_desktop_config.json (or migrate via `claude mcp add-from-claude-desktop`) Migrate github server from PAT+stdio to HTTP transport with OAuth: claude mcp add --transport http github https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ --scope user Then run `/mcp` to complete OAuth flow. Remove the PAT-based npx entry.
3. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/settings.json (env block) Add: "MAX_MCP_OUTPUT_TOKENS": "50000" Rationale: crawler pipelines query Neon Postgres and may return large result sets.
4. CREATE /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/.claude-plugin/.mcp.json (or add `mcpServers` inline in .claude-plugin/plugin.json) Bundle the neon-postgres MCP server with the plugin so it activates automatically on plugin enable, removing the need for per-surface manual setup: { "mcpServers": { "neon": { "type": "http", "url": "...", ... } } }
5. CONSIDER creating /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-mcp.json for the approved server set (filesystem, github, memory, neon) to enforce consistency across all 5 surfaces. Requires admin privileges; defer if single-user.

audit.aligned:
- /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/.claude-plugin/plugin.json exists with required fields: `name`, `description`, `version`, `author`, `repository` — all correctly shaped. - `settings.json` at plugin root uses the supported `agent` key (`"agent": "platform-agent"`) to activate a custom agent on plugin enable — matches the documented pattern exactly. - `.lsp.json` at plugin root correctly bundles 8 language servers (TypeScript, Python, Rust, Go, Lua, Bash, TOML, YAML) following the documented LSP plugin schema. - Plugin structure is correct: `.claude-plugin/` contains only `plugin.json`; `skills/`, `agents/`, `.lsp.json`, `settings.json` are at plugin root as docs require.
- `extraKnownMarketplaces` in settings.json registers 5 marketplaces correctly: claude-plugins-official, knowledge-work-plugins, anthropic-agent-skills, neon, netlify-context-and-tools — all using `{ "source": "github", "repo": "owner/repo" }` format as documented. - `enabledPlugins` activates 5 plugins across those marketplaces: document-skills, example-skills, neon-postgres, pyright-lsp, netlify-skills. - `pyright-lsp@claude-plugins-official` installed from official marketplace — matches the recommended approach for pre-built LSP plugins (docs: "install from official marketplace" for Python/TypeScript/Rust/Go). - versions.json `vendor` section tracks `anthropics/claude-code` by hash + date, matching the marketplace source for `anthropic-agent-skills`.
- Custom `.lsp.json` bundles 8 language servers beyond what the official marketplace covers (Bash, TOML, YAML, Lua not in the official list), which is exactly the documented use case for custom LSP plugins. - versions.json `lsp` section pins all 8 binary versions — good operational hygiene that the docs recommend ("binary required" per language).
audit.gaps:
- No `pluginAccess` key anywhere in settings.json. Docs describe scope-based plugin installation (user/project/local/managed). Current `enabledPlugins` is a flat map with no scope annotation — unclear which plugins are user-scoped vs project-scoped. Project-scope plugins should be installed via `.claude/settings.json` so teammates auto-get them on repo trust. - No `extraKnownMarketplaces` at project level (`.claude/settings.json` in repo root does not exist). Docs recommend adding `extraKnownMarketplaces` there for team marketplace propagation. Currently it lives only in the bootstrap/global settings.
- `typescript-lsp@claude-plugins-official` NOT in enabledPlugins despite TypeScript being the primary language. Instead, the custom `.lsp.json` bundles `typescript- language-server` directly. This is valid but bypasses official plugin lifecycle (auto-update, error reporting via `/plugin` Errors tab). Same applies to `rust-analyzer-lsp`, `gopls-lsp`. - `github@claude-plugins-official` NOT installed. Docs list it as a first-class "External integrations" plugin that bundles a pre-configured MCP server + commands, which would eliminate the manual PAT-based github server in claude_desktop_config.json. - `commit-commands@claude-plugins-official` (or `anthropics-claude-code`) NOT in enabledPlugins. The CLAUDE.md defines git workflow rules but no commit skill is bundled. The vendor/anthropics/claude-code/plugins/commit-commands plugin is vendored but not wired up via marketplace install. - `pr-review-toolkit@claude-plugins-official` NOT installed. Docs describe it as a specialized agent for PR review — relevant given the CI/github-action workflows. - `agent-sdk-dev@claude-plugins-official` NOT installed. Repo imports anthropic SDK (versions.json vendors both Python and TypeScript SDKs). This plugin adds SDK-specific tools and agents. - `sentry@claude-plugins-official` NOT installed. Not in enabledPlugins or extraKnownMarketplaces despite being listed under "External integrations."
- No explicit auto-update configuration documented. Docs say official marketplace plugins have auto-update enabled by default; third-party (neon, netlify) have it disabled. No `FORCE_AUTOUPDATE_PLUGINS` or `DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER` env var set — implicit reliance on defaults. - No `/reload-plugins` hook in SessionStart or ConfigChange hooks. Docs recommend running `/reload-plugins` after plugin changes; having a hook trigger it on ConfigChange would automate this.
- The platform-manager plugin has no `hooks/hooks.json`. The `hooks` section in settings.json (PostToolUse, SessionStart, etc.) is not bundled with the plugin — means hooks don't travel with plugin installs. Docs show `hooks/hooks.json` as a supported plugin component. - No `commands/` directory in plugin root. All custom commands in CLAUDE.md (`/check-cli-docs`, `/sdk-scaffold`, etc.) are defined as CLAUDE.md text, not as proper plugin commands or skills. Docs: "commands/ at plugin root" for slash commands. - Plugin not submitted to any marketplace. versions.json shows it at a GitHub repo (`jadecli/platform-manager`) but no marketplace.json exists in the repo, so `/plugin install platform-manager@...` is not possible for other surfaces. - No `--plugin-dir` development workflow documented. Docs recommend `claude --plugin-dir ./platform-manager` for local iteration; no script or Makefile target exists for this.

audit.aligned:
- All 5 skills (dotfile-audit, repo-context, scrapy-sdk, surface-config, surface-init) have SKILL.md with valid YAML frontmatter between `---` markers. - `name` field present and lowercase-hyphenated in all skills. - `description` field present in all skills with contextual "Use when..." language that helps Claude decide when to auto-invoke — matches the recommended description format. - `effort: low` used in repo-context and surface-init — correct use of the effort frontmatter field for lightweight context-loading skills. - Supporting files pattern used correctly: scrapy-sdk has a `references/` subdirectory; surface-config has a `scripts/` subdirectory — matches "Add supporting files" pattern. - agents/platform-agent.md follows the subagent spec: `name`, `description`, `model`, `initialPrompt`, `tools` all present.
- dotfile-audit, surface-init, surface-config do NOT set `disable-model-invocation: true`, which is intentional — Claude can load them when contextually relevant (audit on drift detection, init on session start, config on surface setup questions). Appropriate default. - repo-context and surface-init both use `effort: low`, correctly signaling low-cost auto-loading.
- Each skill is a directory containing SKILL.md as entrypoint — matches the required `skill-name/SKILL.md` structure exactly. - Plugin skills are at plugin root `skills/` (not inside `.claude-plugin/`) — correct per the "Common mistake" warning in docs.
audit.gaps:
- NONE of the 5 skills use `disable-model-invocation: true`. While appropriate for most, surface-config and scrapy-sdk have side effects (writes config files, runs scrapy commands). These should be protected from accidental auto-invocation. Docs: "Use for workflows you want to trigger manually... /deploy, /send-slack-message." - No skill uses `allowed-tools` to restrict tool access. scrapy-sdk invokes Bash to run scrapy commands; restricting to `Bash(scrapy *), Read, Write` would prevent accidental broad tool use during skill execution. - No skill uses `context: fork` for subagent isolation. dotfile-audit reads external XML files and compares state — this is a read-heavy research task ideal for `context: fork` with `agent: Explore` to avoid polluting main conversation context. - No skill uses `user-invocable: false`. The `surface-init` skill is background knowledge (loads at session start automatically); hiding it from the `/` menu with `user-invocable: false` would reduce noise while keeping Claude's auto-invoke working. - No skill uses `argument-hint` in frontmatter. scrapy-sdk and repo-context accept implicit arguments (URLs, repo names) but provide no autocomplete hint. Docs: `argument-hint: "[repo-url]"` improves DX. - No skill uses `model` override. repo-context and surface-init are low-effort reads that could be pinned to a faster/cheaper model (e.g. `model: haiku`) to save tokens on frequent auto-invocations.
- No `surface-audit` skill (compare all 5 surfaces' settings for drift). The dotfile-audit skill only covers the local surface; a cross-surface audit is described in the platform-agent but not packaged as an invocable skill. - No skill wraps the `/check-cli-docs`, `/check-platform-docs`, `/sdk-scaffold` commands referenced in CLAUDE.md. These are stubs in prose, not actual SKILL.md files. They should be promoted to skills with proper frontmatter. - No `session-logger` or session-summary skill despite a SessionEnd hook in settings.json that calls session-lifecycle.sh. A skill that logs or summarizes the session would complement the hook.
- scrapy-sdk/SKILL.md references TypeScript CLI (`@jadecli/scrapy-cli`) but does not use `!`command`` dynamic context injection to pre-fetch available spider list or validate CLI presence before Claude acts. Docs: `!`command`` runs preprocessing before Claude sees the prompt. - dotfile-audit/SKILL.md references absolute paths (`/Users/alexzh/jadecli-ecosystem/shared/context/dotfiles.xml`) hardcoded in the skill body. Should use `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}` substitution or relative path patterns to remain portable across surfaces. - surface-init/SKILL.md also hardcodes `/Users/alexzh/jadecli-ecosystem/` paths. Same portability concern. - No skill in the plugin uses `hooks` frontmatter field for skill-scoped lifecycle hooks (e.g. a PostToolUse hook scoped only to scrapy-sdk to validate generated spider files).
- 5 plugin skills + no budget override. If more skills are added (e.g. after converting CLAUDE.md commands), the 16,000-char default budget may be exceeded. No `SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET` env var set in settings.json. - `bootstrap/claude-config/skills/` directory does not exist (Glob returned nothing). The bootstrap config package has settings, hooks, and commands stubs in CLAUDE.md but no standalone skills separate from the plugin. - `bootstrap/claude-config/commands/` directory does not exist. All custom commands are embedded in CLAUDE.md prose, not in discoverable Markdown files.
- platform-agent.md uses `model: sonnet` (short alias). Docs show model field accepts full model IDs. The alias may resolve correctly but should be verified against the supported values list; prefer explicit `claude-sonnet-4-5` or similar. - platform-agent.md has no `effort` field. Given it reads multiple XML files at startup (`initialPrompt` triggers reads), `effort: medium` would be appropriate.
audit.actions:
1. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/surface-config/SKILL.md Add to frontmatter: disable-model-invocation: true allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash(bash scripts/*) Rationale: writes config files; should only run on explicit user invocation.
2. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/scrapy-sdk/SKILL.md Add to frontmatter: disable-model-invocation: true allowed-tools: Read, Write, Bash(scrapy *), Bash(npx @jadecli/scrapy-cli *) argument-hint: "[spider-name] [target-url]" Also add `!`npx @jadecli/scrapy-cli list 2>/dev/null`` inline context injection so Claude sees available spiders before acting.
3. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/dotfile-audit/SKILL.md Add to frontmatter: context: fork agent: Explore allowed-tools: Read, Glob, Grep disable-model-invocation: true Replace hardcoded /Users/alexzh paths with ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}-relative references or parametric $ARGUMENTS for surface root.
4. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/surface-init/SKILL.md Add to frontmatter: user-invocable: false Rationale: background loader; not a user-invocable action. Claude should auto-load it; it should not appear in the `/` menu. Also replace hardcoded /Users/alexzh/jadecli-ecosystem paths with $ARGUMENTS[0] fallback or env-var injection.
5. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/repo-context/SKILL.md Add to frontmatter: model: claude-haiku-4-5 argument-hint: "[repo-path-or-url]" Rationale: pure read task; haiku is sufficient and saves tokens on frequent auto-invocations when entering new repos.
6. CREATE /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/skills/check-cli-docs/SKILL.md Convert the /check-cli-docs CLAUDE.md stub into a proper skill: --- name: check-cli-docs description: Fetches and summarizes Claude Code CLI documentation for a topic. context: fork agent: Explore allowed-tools: WebFetch, Read argument-hint: "[topic]" --- (Repeat pattern for /sdk-scaffold, /structured-output, /tool-definition)
7. MODIFY /Users/alexzh/platform-manager/bootstrap/claude-config/settings.json (env block) Add: "SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET": "32000" Rationale: 5 current skills + new ones from action 6 will approach the 16k default.
